source: OET Writing assessment criteria (derived from R16.1–R16.8 + official OET documentation)

bands.3: Purpose is immediately apparent. Clearly conveys the reason for writing including the specific request/action required.
bands.2: Purpose is apparent and generally fit-for-intent. Includes the reason and request with only minor lack of clarity.
bands.1: Purpose is somewhat apparent but poorly served. Reader may need to infer the reason or request.

criteria.title: Content
criteria.description: What information is included: relevant details included, irrelevant details excluded, accurate and specific.

criteria.title: Conciseness & Clarity
criteria.description: How efficiently information is communicated; free of redundancy and ambiguity.

criteria.title: Genre & Style
criteria.description: Appropriate register, tone, conventions of the specific letter type.

criteria.title: Organisation & Layout
criteria.description: Clear structure: paragraphing, sequencing, layout conventions (address, date, salutation, closure).

criteria.title: Language
criteria.description: Accuracy and range of grammar, vocabulary, spelling, punctuation.

targetBands.note: Required approximately Band 6+ in most criteria; at least Band 2 out of 3 in Purpose.

weighting.realClinicTest: Could this letter be sent in a real clinical setting? Yes = pass. No = fail.
